text:
Every year, on a Saturday evening by the firepit in our small backwoods cabin, Jenny tells us a story from her childhood. Our pets, a curious bluejay and a sleepy owl, listen attentively, their eyes big with awe.

Once upon a time, there was a quirky old owl named Old Wiley. He didn't go out much. You see, aside from keeping all the other owls updated on news from high above, Old Wiley had a life limited to his cozy nest upstairs. He couldn't move much, could hardly talk, but everyone knew he loved collecting shiny stones — nothing else mattered to him.

After several weeks of nothing but shadows and stones, word spread amongst the animal kingdom that Old Wiley had discovered something miraculous. Not stones. Real things. Gems. Hundreds of them! They were all mixed together, like a clumsy miner's collection.

One lazy afternoon, after some bird-brains had discovered Old Wiley's cavernous treasure trove, a squirrel decided to take
